     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Startup Brief Builder
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Phase 026: Build a compact startup brief for hook-capable runtimes
     5	// from local code graph + hook state (no MCP round-trip).
     6	
     7	import { getStats, queryStartupHighlights, type StartupHighlight } from './code-graph-db.js';
     8	import { getGraphFreshness } from './ensure-ready.js';
     9	import { loadMostRecentState } from '../../hooks/claude/hook-state.js';
    10	import { isCocoIndexAvailable } from '../utils/cocoindex-path.js';
    11	import {
    12	  createSharedPayloadEnvelope,
    13	  trustStateFromGraphState,
    14	  type SharedPayloadEnvelope,
    15	  type SharedPayloadSection,
    16	} from '../context/shared-payload.js';
    17	
    18	/** Compact startup summary for the locally indexed code graph. */
    19	export interface StartupGraphSummary {
    20	  files: number;
    21	  nodes: number;
    22	  edges: number;
    23	  lastScan: string | null;
    24	}
    25	
    26	/** Startup brief payload returned to hook-capable runtimes at session start. */
    27	export interface StartupBriefResult {
    28	  graphOutline: string | null;
    29	  sessionContinuity: string | null;
    30	  graphSummary: StartupGraphSummary | null;
    31	  graphState: 'ready' | 'stale' | 'empty' | 'missing';
    32	  cocoIndexAvailable: boolean;
    33	  startupSurface: string;
    34	  sharedPayload: SharedPayloadEnvelope | null;
    35	}
    36	
    37	const SUMMARY_MAX_CHARS = 240;
    38	
    39	function truncateInline(text: string, maxChars: number): string {
    40	  const normalized = text.replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
    41	  if (normalized.length <= maxChars) {
    42	    return normalized;
    43	  }
    44	  return normalized.slice(0, maxChars - 3).trimEnd() + '...';
    45	}
    46	
    47	function compactPath(filePath: string): string {
    48	  const parts = filePath.replace(/\\/g, '/').split('/').filter(Boolean);
    49	  if (parts.length <= 3) {
    50	    return parts.join('/');
    51	  }
    52	  return parts.slice(-3).join('/');
    53	}
    54	
    55	function formatHighlight(highlight: StartupHighlight): string {
    56	  const callSuffix = highlight.callCount > 0 ? ` [calls: ${highlight.callCount}]` : '';
    57	  return `- ${highlight.name} (${highlight.kind}) - ${compactPath(highlight.filePath)}${callSuffix}`;
    58	}
    59	
    60	function formatCompactNumber(value: number): string {
    61	  return new Intl.NumberFormat('en-US', {
    62	    notation: value >= 1000 ? 'compact' : 'standard',
    63	    maximumFractionDigits: value >= 1000 ? 1 : 0,
    64	  }).format(value);
    65	}
    66	
    67	function describeLastScan(lastScan: string | null): string {
    68	  if (!lastScan) {
    69	    return 'last scan unknown';
    70	  }
    71	
    72	  const scanDate = new Date(lastScan);
    73	  if (Number.isNaN(scanDate.getTime())) {
    74	    return 'last scan unknown';
    75	  }
    76	
    77	  const now = new Date();
    78	  const sameDay = scanDate.getFullYear() === now.getFullYear()
    79	    && scanDate.getMonth() === now.getMonth()
    80	    && scanDate.getDate() === now.getDate();
    81	  if (sameDay) {
    82	    return 'scanned today';
    83	  }
    84	
    85	  return `last scan ${scanDate.toISOString().slice(0, 10)}`;
    86	}
    87	
    88	function buildStartupSurface(args: {
    89	  sessionContinuity: string | null;
    90	  graphSummary: StartupGraphSummary | null;
    91	  graphState: StartupBriefResult['graphState'];
    92	  cocoIndexAvailable: boolean;
    93	}): string {
    94	  const memoryLine = args.sessionContinuity
    95	    ? 'session continuity available'
    96	    : 'startup summary only (resume on demand)';
    97	
    98	  let codeGraphLine = 'unavailable';
    99	  if (args.graphState === 'ready' && args.graphSummary) {
   100	    codeGraphLine = [
   101	      'healthy',
   102	      `${args.graphSummary.files.toLocaleString('en-US')} files`,
   103	      `${formatCompactNumber(args.graphSummary.nodes)} nodes`,
   104	      `${formatCompactNumber(args.graphSummary.edges)} edges`,
   105	      `(${describeLastScan(args.graphSummary.lastScan)})`,
   106	    ].join(' -- ').replace(' -- (', ' (');
   107	  } else if (args.graphState === 'stale' && args.graphSummary) {
   108	    codeGraphLine = [
   109	      'stale',
   110	      `${args.graphSummary.files.toLocaleString('en-US')} files`,
   111	      `${formatCompactNumber(args.graphSummary.nodes)} nodes`,
   112	      `${formatCompactNumber(args.graphSummary.edges)} edges`,
   113	      `(${describeLastScan(args.graphSummary.lastScan)}; first structural read may refresh inline)`,
   114	    ].join(' -- ').replace(' -- (', ' (');
   115	  } else if (args.graphState === 'empty') {
   116	    codeGraphLine = 'empty -- run `code_graph_scan`';
   117	  }
   118	
   119	  return [
   120	    'Session context received. Current state:',
   121	    '',
   122	    `- Memory: ${memoryLine}`,
   123	    `- Code Graph: ${codeGraphLine}`,
   124	    `- CocoIndex: ${args.cocoIndexAvailable ? 'available' : 'missing'}`,
   125	    '- Note: this is a startup snapshot; later structural reads may differ if the repo state changed.',
   126	    '',
   127	    'What would you like to work on?',
   128	  ].join('\n');
   129	}
   130	
   131	function buildGraphOutline(highlightCount: number = 5): Pick<StartupBriefResult, 'graphOutline' | 'graphSummary' | 'graphState'> {
   132	  try {
   133	    const stats = getStats();
   134	    const freshness = getGraphFreshness(process.cwd());
   135	    const graphSummary: StartupGraphSummary = {
   136	      files: stats.totalFiles,
   137	      nodes: stats.totalNodes,
   138	      edges: stats.totalEdges,
   139	      lastScan: stats.lastScanTimestamp,
   140	    };
   141	
   142	    if (stats.totalFiles <= 0 || stats.totalNodes <= 0) {
   143	      return {
   144	        graphOutline: null,
   145	        graphSummary,
   146	        graphState: 'empty',
   147	      };
   148	    }
   149	
   150	    // Keep startup highlights small by default so graph context stays useful without crowding the token budget.
   151	    const highlights = queryStartupHighlights(highlightCount);
   152	    const lines: string[] = [
   153	      `${stats.totalFiles} files, ${stats.totalNodes} nodes, ${stats.totalEdges} edges.`,
   154	      `Last scan: ${stats.lastScanTimestamp ?? 'unknown'}`,
   155	    ];
   156	    if (freshness === 'stale') {
   157	      lines.push('Freshness: stale — first structural read may trigger bounded inline refresh or recommend code_graph_scan.');
   158	    }
   159	    if (highlights.length > 0) {
   160	      lines.push('Orientation: use code graph highlights for structural entry points and call paths; use CocoIndex for semantic discovery when the symbol or file is still unknown.');
   161	      lines.push('Highlights:');
   162	      lines.push(...highlights.map(formatHighlight));
   163	    }
   164	
   165	    return {
   166	      graphOutline: lines.join('\n'),
   167	      graphSummary,
   168	      graphState: freshness === 'stale' ? 'stale' : 'ready',
   169	    };
   170	  } catch {
   171	    return {
   172	      graphOutline: null,
   173	      graphSummary: null,
   174	      graphState: 'missing',
   175	    };
   176	  }
   177	}
   178	
   179	function buildSessionContinuity(): string | null {
   180	  const state = loadMostRecentState();
   181	  if (!state) {
   182	    return null;
   183	  }
   184	
   185	  const parts: string[] = [];
   186	  if (state.lastSpecFolder) {
   187	    parts.push(`Last session worked on: ${state.lastSpecFolder}`);
   188	  }
   189	  if (state.sessionSummary?.text) {
   190	    parts.push(`Summary: ${truncateInline(state.sessionSummary.text, SUMMARY_MAX_CHARS)}`);
   191	  }
   192	  return parts.length > 0 ? parts.join('\n') : null;
   193	}
   194	
   195	/** Build the startup brief used by runtime hooks and transport startup digests. */
   196	export function buildStartupBrief(highlightCount?: number): StartupBriefResult {
   197	  const graph = buildGraphOutline(highlightCount);
   198	  const sessionContinuity = buildSessionContinuity();
   199	  const cocoIndexAvailable = isCocoIndexAvailable();
   200	  const sections: SharedPayloadSection[] = [];
   201	  if (graph.graphOutline) {
   202	    sections.push({
   203	      key: 'structural-context',
   204	      title: 'Structural Context',
   205	      content: graph.graphOutline,
   206	      source: 'code-graph',
   207	    });
   208	  }
   209	  if (sessionContinuity) {
   210	    sections.push({
   211	      key: 'session-continuity',
   212	      title: 'Session Continuity',
   213	      content: sessionContinuity,
   214	      source: 'session',
   215	    });
   216	  }
   217	
   218	  const startupSurface = buildStartupSurface({
   219	    sessionContinuity,
   220	    graphSummary: graph.graphSummary,
   221	    graphState: graph.graphState,
   222	    cocoIndexAvailable,
   223	  });
   224	
   225	  const sharedPayload = sections.length > 0
   226	    ? createSharedPayloadEnvelope({
   227	      kind: 'startup',
   228	      sections,
   229	      summary: graph.graphOutline
   230	        ? `Startup brief with ${graph.graphState} structural context and ${sessionContinuity ? 'session continuity' : 'no continuity notes'}`
   231	        : `Startup brief without structural highlights (${graph.graphState})`,
   232	      provenance: {
   233	        producer: 'startup_brief',
   234	        sourceSurface: 'session_start',
   235	        trustState: trustStateFromGraphState(graph.graphState),
   236	        generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
   237	        lastUpdated: graph.graphSummary?.lastScan ?? null,
   238	        sourceRefs: ['code-graph-db', 'hook-state'],
   239	      },
   240	    })
   241	    : null;
   242	
   243	  return {
   244	    graphOutline: graph.graphOutline,
   245	    sessionContinuity,
   246	    graphSummary: graph.graphSummary,
   247	    graphState: graph.graphState,
   248	    cocoIndexAvailable,
   249	    startupSurface,
   250	    sharedPayload,
   251	  };
   252	}

     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Session Bootstrap Handler
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Phase 024 / Item 7: Composite tool that runs session_resume
     5	// + session_health in one call, merging results with hints.
     6	
     7	import { handleSessionResume } from './session-resume.js';
     8	import type { CachedSessionSummaryDecision } from './session-resume.js';
     9	import { handleSessionHealth } from './session-health.js';
    10	import { recordBootstrapEvent } from '../lib/session/context-metrics.js';
    11	import { buildStructuralBootstrapContract } from '../lib/session/session-snapshot.js';
    12	import type { StructuralBootstrapContract } from '../lib/session/session-snapshot.js';
    13	import {
    14	  attachStructuralTrustFields,
    15	  buildStructuralContextTrust,
    16	  createSharedPayloadEnvelope,
    17	  summarizeUnknown,
    18	  summarizeCertaintyContract,
    19	  trustStateFromStructuralStatus,
    20	  type SharedPayloadCertainty,
    21	  type SharedPayloadEnvelope,
    22	  type SharedPayloadSection,
    23	  StructuralTrustPayloadError,
    24	  type StructuralTrust,
    25	} from '../lib/context/shared-payload.js';
    26	import {
    27	  buildOpenCodeTransportPlan,
    28	  coerceSharedPayloadEnvelope,
    29	  type OpenCodeTransportPlan,
    30	} from '../lib/context/opencode-transport.js';
    31	import {
    32	  buildCodeGraphOpsContract,
    33	  type CodeGraphOpsContract,
    34	} from '../lib/code-graph/ops-hardening.js';
    35	import type { MCPResponse } from '@spec-kit/shared/types';
    36	
    37	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    38	   1. TYPES
    39	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    40	
    41	interface SessionBootstrapArgs {
    42	  specFolder?: string;
    43	}
    44	
    45	interface SessionBootstrapResult {
    46	  resume: Record<string, unknown>;
    47	  health: Record<string, unknown>;
    48	  cachedSummary?: CachedSessionSummaryDecision;
    49	  structuralContext?: StructuralBootstrapContract & StructuralTrust;
    50	  structuralRoutingNudge?: {
    51	    advisory: true;
    52	    readiness: 'ready';
    53	    preferredTool: 'code_graph_query';
    54	    message: string;
    55	    preservesAuthority: 'session_bootstrap';
    56	  };
    57	  payloadContract?: SharedPayloadEnvelope;
    58	  opencodeTransport?: OpenCodeTransportPlan;
    59	  graphOps?: CodeGraphOpsContract;
    60	  hints: string[];
    61	  nextActions: string[];
    62	}
    63	
    64	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    65	   2. HELPERS
    66	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    67	
    68	function extractData(response: MCPResponse): Record<string, unknown> {
    69	  try {
    70	    const text = response?.content?.[0]?.text;
    71	    if (typeof text === 'string') {
    72	      const parsed = JSON.parse(text);
    73	      return parsed?.data ?? parsed ?? {};
    74	    }
    75	  } catch { /* parse failed */ }
    76	  return {};
    77	}
    78	
    79	function extractHints(data: Record<string, unknown>): string[] {
    80	  if (Array.isArray(data.hints)) return data.hints as string[];
    81	  return [];
    82	}
    83	
    84	function extractCachedSummary(
    85	  data: Record<string, unknown>,
    86	): CachedSessionSummaryDecision | null {
    87	  const candidate = data.cachedSummary;
    88	  if (candidate && typeof candidate === 'object') {
    89	    return candidate as CachedSessionSummaryDecision;
    90	  }
    91	  return null;
    92	}
    93	
    94	function buildNextActions(
    95	  resumeData: Record<string, unknown>,
    96	  healthData: Record<string, unknown>,
    97	  structuralContext: StructuralBootstrapContract,
    98	): string[] {
    99	  const nextActions = new Set<string>();
   100	
   101	  if (resumeData.error) {
   102	    nextActions.add('Call `session_resume({ specFolder })` directly to inspect the detailed resume failure.');
   103	  }
   104	
   105	  if (healthData.error) {
   106	    nextActions.add('Call `session_health()` directly to inspect the current health-check failure.');
   107	  }
   108	
   109	  if (structuralContext.recommendedAction) {
   110	    nextActions.add(structuralContext.recommendedAction);
   111	  }
   112	
   113	  if (structuralContext.status === 'ready') {
   114	    nextActions.add('Use `session_resume({ specFolder })` when you need the fuller merged recovery payload.');
   115	  } else if (structuralContext.status === 'stale') {
   116	    nextActions.add('Run `code_graph_scan` if you need fresh structural context, then call `session_bootstrap()` again.');
   117	  } else {
   118	    nextActions.add('If structural context matters for this task, run `code_graph_scan` and then re-run `session_bootstrap()`.');
   119	  }
   120	
   121	  const healthStatus = typeof healthData.status === 'string' ? healthData.status : null;
   122	  if (healthStatus === 'warning' || healthStatus === 'stale') {
   123	    nextActions.add('Call `memory_context({ input: "resume previous work", mode: "resume", profile: "resume" })` if you need a deeper state refresh.');
   124	  }
   125	
   126	  return [...nextActions].slice(0, 3);
   127	}
   128	
   129	function extractStructuralTrustFromPayload(
   130	  payload: SharedPayloadEnvelope | null,
   131	): StructuralTrust | null {
   132	  if (!payload) {
   133	    return null;
   134	  }
   135	
   136	  const structuralSection = payload.sections.find((section) =>
   137	    section.key === 'structural-context' && section.structuralTrust,
   138	  );
   139	
   140	  return structuralSection?.structuralTrust ?? null;
   141	}
   142	
   143	function buildStructuralRoutingNudge(
   144	  structuralContext: StructuralBootstrapContract,
   145	): SessionBootstrapResult['structuralRoutingNudge'] | null {
   146	  if (structuralContext.status !== 'ready') {
   147	    return null;
   148	  }
   149	
   150	  return {
   151	    advisory: true,
   152	    readiness: 'ready',
   153	    preferredTool: 'code_graph_query',
   154	    message: 'Advisory only: when the next question is about callers, imports, dependencies, or outline, prefer `code_graph_query` before Grep or Glob.',
   155	    preservesAuthority: 'session_bootstrap',
   156	  };
   157	}
   158	
   159	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   160	   3. HANDLER
   161	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   162	
   163	/** Handle session_bootstrap tool call — one-call session setup */
   164	export async function handleSessionBootstrap(args: SessionBootstrapArgs): Promise<MCPResponse> {
   165	  const startMs = Date.now();
   166	  const allHints: string[] = [];
   167	
   168	  // Sub-call 1: session_resume with full resume payload
   169	  let resumeData: Record<string, unknown> = {};
   170	  try {
   171	    const resumeResponse = await handleSessionResume({
   172	      specFolder: args.specFolder,
   173	    });
   174	    resumeData = extractData(resumeResponse);
   175	    allHints.push(...extractHints(resumeData));
   176	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   177	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   178	    resumeData = { error: message };
   179	    allHints.push('session_resume failed. Try calling it manually.');
   180	  }
   181	
   182	  // Sub-call 2: session_health
   183	  let healthData: Record<string, unknown> = {};
   184	  try {
   185	    const healthResponse = await handleSessionHealth();
   186	    healthData = extractData(healthResponse);
   187	    allHints.push(...extractHints(healthData));
   188	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   189	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   190	    healthData = { error: message };
   191	    allHints.push('session_health failed. Try calling it manually.');
   192	  }
   193	
   194	  // Phase 027: Structural bootstrap contract
   195	  const structuralContext = buildStructuralBootstrapContract('session_bootstrap');
   196	  if (structuralContext.status === 'stale' || structuralContext.status === 'missing') {
   197	    allHints.push(
   198	      `Structural context is ${structuralContext.status}. Run code_graph_scan if needed, then re-run session_bootstrap.`
   199	    );
   200	  }
   201	
   202	  const cachedSummary = extractCachedSummary(resumeData);
   203	  if (cachedSummary?.status === 'accepted') {
   204	    allHints.push('Cached continuity summary accepted as additive bootstrap context.');
   205	  }
   206	  const structuralRoutingNudge = buildStructuralRoutingNudge(structuralContext);
   207	  if (structuralRoutingNudge) {
   208	    allHints.push(structuralRoutingNudge.message);
   209	  }
   210	
   211	  // Deduplicate hints
   212	  const uniqueHints = [...new Set(allHints)];
   213	
   214	  // Record bootstrap telemetry once for the composite call.
   215	  const durationMs = Date.now() - startMs;
   216	  const completeness = resumeData.error || healthData.error ? 'partial' : 'full';
   217	  recordBootstrapEvent('tool', durationMs, completeness);
   218	
   219	  const resumeCertainty: SharedPayloadCertainty = resumeData.error ? 'unknown' : 'estimated';
   220	  const healthCertainty: SharedPayloadCertainty = healthData.error ? 'unknown' : 'estimated';
   221	  const cachedCertainty: SharedPayloadCertainty = cachedSummary?.status === 'accepted' ? 'estimated' : 'defaulted';
   222	  const structuralCertainty: SharedPayloadCertainty = 'exact';
   223	  const nextActionsCertainty: SharedPayloadCertainty = 'defaulted';
   224	  const resumePayload = coerceSharedPayloadEnvelope(resumeData.payloadContract);
   225	  const healthPayload = coerceSharedPayloadEnvelope(healthData.payloadContract);
   226	  const structuralSnapshotTrust = buildStructuralContextTrust(structuralContext);
   227	  const resumeStructuralTrust = extractStructuralTrustFromPayload(resumePayload);
   228	  if (!resumeData.error && !resumeStructuralTrust) {
   229	    throw new StructuralTrustPayloadError(
   230	      'session_bootstrap expected session_resume to emit structural-context.structuralTrust.',
   231	    );
   232	  }
   233	  const structuralContextWithTrust = attachStructuralTrustFields(
   234	    structuralContext,
   235	    structuralSnapshotTrust,
   236	    { label: 'session_bootstrap structural context payload' },
   237	  );
   238	  const resumeWithTrust = resumeData.error
   239	    ? resumeData
   240	    : attachStructuralTrustFields(
   241	      resumeData,
   242	      resumeStructuralTrust,
   243	      { label: 'session_bootstrap resume payload' },
   244	    );
   245	
   246	  const payloadSections: SharedPayloadSection[] = [
   247	    {
   248	      key: 'resume-surface',
   249	      title: 'Resume Surface',
   250	      content: summarizeUnknown(resumeData),
   251	      source: 'memory',
   252	      certainty: resumeCertainty,
   253	    },
   254	  ];
   255	  if (cachedSummary?.status === 'accepted' && cachedSummary.cachedSummary) {
   256	    payloadSections.push({
   257	      key: 'cached-continuity',
   258	      title: 'Cached Continuity',
   259	      content: [
   260	        cachedSummary.cachedSummary.continuityText,

Capture the evidence needed to prove the layer definitions module enumerates L1-L7 budgets, priorities, use-case guidance, and tool memberships; layer IDs still map to task types for recommendation metadata; runtime dispatch remains a name-based hop rather than a seven-layer router; and recommended layer hints surface without changing the underlying dispatch path. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.`
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/14--pipeline-architecture/205-7-layer-tool-architecture-metadata.md:29:| 205 | 7-layer tool architecture metadata | Verify the 7-layer model supplies advisory budgets and layer recommendations while runtime dispatch still routes tools by name through the existing dispatcher modules | `Validate the 7-layer tool architecture metadata. Capture the evidence needed to prove the layer definitions module enumerates L1-L7 budgets, priorities, use-case guidance, and tool memberships; layer IDs still map to task types for recommendation metadata; runtime dispatch remains a name-based hop rather than a seven-layer router; and recommended layer hints surface without changing the underlying dispatch path. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) Review the layer-definitions module and confirm all seven layers expose token budgets, priorities, guidance, and tool membership metadata 2) Verify layer IDs still map to task-type recommendation hints 3) Trace a representative tool invocation through the runtime entrypoint and confirm dispatch occurs by tool name into the existing dispatcher modules 4) Exercise or inspect one response path that surfaces recommended-layer metadata 5) Confirm that changing or reading layer metadata does not alter the fundamental name-based dispatch hop | L1-L7 metadata includes budgets/priorities/guidance/tool membership; task-type mappings remain available for recommendations; runtime dispatch is still name-based and fans into the existing dispatcher modules; recommended-layer metadata is advisory rather than a routing prerequisite | Layer-definition snapshot + dispatch trace + recommended-layer output example + comparison showing runtime path remains name-based | PASS if the layer model behaves as governance metadata and dispatch remains name-based; FAIL if runtime behavior depends on a missing seven-layer router or if metadata/recommendation claims do not match the actual dispatch implementation | Inspect `layer-definitions.ts` completeness; verify `context-server.ts` dispatch entrypoint and budget injection; review `tools/index.ts` fan-out modules; confirm handler metadata does not masquerade as an execution router |

   851	function touchEdgeAccess(edgeId: number): void {
   852	  if (!db) return;
   853	  (db.prepare(
   854	    "UPDATE causal_edges SET last_accessed = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?"
   855	  ) as Database.Statement).run(edgeId);

   857	
   858	function getStaleEdges(thresholdDays: number = STALENESS_THRESHOLD_DAYS): CausalEdge[] {
   859	  if (!db) return [];
   860	  try {
   861	    return (db.prepare(`
   862	      SELECT * FROM causal_edges
   863	      WHERE (last_accessed IS NULL AND extracted_at < datetime('now', '-' || ? || ' days'))
   864	         OR (last_accessed IS NOT NULL AND last_accessed < datetime('now', '-' || ? || ' days'))
   865	      ORDER BY COALESCE(last_accessed, extracted_at) ASC
   866	    `) as Database.Statement).all(thresholdDays, thresholdDays) as CausalEdge[];
   867	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   868	    const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   869	    console.warn(`[causal-edges] getStaleEdges error: ${msg}`);
   870	    return [];
   871	  }
   872	}

- symptom: validator flags metadata anchor/phase headers; cause: template divergence during manual edits; fix: restore template sections before content edits [Task 3]
- symptom: `TEMPLATE_HEADERS` validation fails during packet closeout; cause: missing literal `12. OPEN QUESTIONS` header; fix: restore the required heading and rerun `validate.sh --no-recursive` [Task 4]
- symptom: packet docs drift from deep-research conclusions; cause: spec/plan/tasks/checklist/summary edited piecemeal; fix: run a single truth-sync pass across all packet docs with explicit references to `research/research.md` [Task 5]

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Patterns
     2	
     3	This folder contains task-specific guidance — the things you would tell your agent if you were sitting next to it. Not generic instructions. Project-specific accumulated wisdom.
     4	
     5	## How patterns get created
     6	
     7	**During setup:** After the context/ files are populated, the agent generates starter patterns based on the project's actual stack, architecture, and conventions. These are stack-specific — a Flask API project gets different patterns than a React SPA or a CLI tool.
     8	
     9	**Over time:** You or your agent add patterns as they emerge from real work — when something breaks, when a task has a non-obvious gotcha, when you've explained the same thing twice.
    10	
    11	## What belongs here
    12	
    13	A pattern file is worth creating when:
    14	- A task type is common in this project and has a repeatable workflow
    15	- There are integration gotchas between components that aren't obvious from code
    16	- Something broke and you want to prevent it from breaking the same way again
    17	- A verify checklist specific to one type of task would catch mistakes early
    18	
    19	## When to skip a pattern
    20	
    21	Default to generating a pattern. Only skip if:
    22	- The exact same guidance is already in `context/conventions.md` with concrete examples
    23	- The task truly has no project-specific gotchas (e.g. "how to write a for loop")
    24	
    25	If in doubt, generate the pattern. A pattern that turns out to be obvious costs nothing. A missing pattern costs a broken codebase.
    26	
    27	## Format
    28	
    29	### Single-task pattern (one file = one task)
    30	
    31	```markdown
    32	---
    33	name: [pattern-name]
    34	description: [one line — what this pattern covers and when to use it]
    35	triggers:
    36	  - "[keyword that should trigger loading this file]"
    37	edges:
    38	  - target: "[related file path, e.g. context/conventions.md]"
    39	    condition: "[when to follow this edge]"
    40	last_updated: [YYYY-MM-DD]
    41	---
    42	
    43	# [Pattern Name]
    44	
    45	## Context
    46	[What to load or know before starting this task type]
    47	
    48	## Steps
    49	[The workflow — what to do, in what order]
    50	
    51	## Gotchas
    52	[The things that go wrong. What to watch out for.]
    53	
    54	## Verify
    55	[Checklist to run after completing this task type]
    56	
    57	## Debug
    58	[What to check when this task type breaks]
    59	
    60	## Update Scaffold
    61	- [ ] Update `.mex/ROUTER.md` "Current Project State" if what's working/not built has changed
    62	- [ ] Update any `.mex/context/` files that are now out of date
    63	- [ ] If this is a new task type without a pattern, create one in `.mex/patterns/` and add to `INDEX.md`
    64	```
    65	
    66	### Multi-section pattern (one file = multiple related tasks)
    67	
    68	Use this when tasks share context but differ in steps. Each task gets its own
    69	`## Task: ...` heading with sub-sections. The Context section is shared at the top.
    70	
    71	```markdown
    72	---
    73	name: [pattern-name]
    74	description: [one line — what this pattern file covers]
    75	triggers:
    76	  - "[keyword]"
    77	edges:
    78	  - target: "[related file path]"
    79	    condition: "[when to follow this edge]"
    80	last_updated: [YYYY-MM-DD]
    81	---
    82	
    83	# [Pattern Name]
    84	
    85	## Context
    86	[Shared context for all tasks in this file]
    87	
    88	## Task: [First Task Name]
    89	
    90	### Steps
    91	[...]
    92	
    93	### Gotchas
    94	[...]
    95	
    96	### Verify
    97	[...]
    98	
    99	## Task: [Second Task Name]
   100	
   101	### Steps
   102	[...]
   103	
   104	## Update Scaffold
   105	- [ ] Update `.mex/ROUTER.md` "Current Project State" if what's working/not built has changed
   106	- [ ] Update any `.mex/context/` files that are now out of date
   107	- [ ] If this is a new task type without a pattern, create one in `.mex/patterns/` and add to `INDEX.md`
   108	```
   109	
   110	Do NOT combine unrelated tasks into one file just to reduce file count.
   111	Only group tasks that genuinely share context.
   112	
   113	## How many patterns to generate
   114	
   115	Do not use a fixed number. Generate one pattern per:
   116	- Each major task type a developer does repeatedly in this project
   117	- Each external dependency with non-obvious integration gotchas
   118	- Each major failure boundary in the architecture flow
   119	
   120	For a simple project this may be 3-4 files. For a complex project this may be 10-15.
   121	Do not cap based on a number — cap based on whether the pattern adds real value.
   122	
   123	## Pattern categories
   124	
   125	Walk through each category below. For each one, check the relevant context files
   126	and generate patterns for everything that applies to this project.
   127	
   128	### Category 1 — Common task patterns
   129	
   130	The repeatable tasks in this project. What does a developer do most often?
   131	
   132	Derive from: `context/architecture.md` (what are the major components?) and
   133	`context/conventions.md` (what patterns exist for extending them?)
   134	
   135	Examples by project type:
   136	- API: "add new endpoint", "add new model/entity", "add auth to a route"
   137	- Frontend: "add new page/route", "add new component", "add form with validation"
   138	- CLI: "add new command", "add new flag/option"
   139	- Pipeline: "add new pipeline stage", "add new data source"
   140	- SaaS: "add payment flow", "add user-facing feature", "add admin operation"
   141	
   142	### Category 2 — Integration patterns
   143	
   144	How to work with the external dependencies in this project.
   145	
   146	Every entry in `context/stack.md` "Key Libraries" or `context/architecture.md`
   147	"External Dependencies" that has non-obvious setup, gotchas, or failure modes
   148	deserves a pattern. These are the most dangerous areas — the agent will
   149	confidently write integration code that looks right but misses project-specific
   150	configuration, error handling, or rate limiting.
   151	
   152	Examples: "calling the payments API", "running database migrations",
   153	"adding a new third-party service client", "configuring auth provider"
   154	
   155	### Category 3 — Debug/diagnosis patterns
   156	
   157	When something breaks, where do you look?
   158	
   159	Derive from the architecture flow — each boundary between components is a
   160	potential failure point. One debug pattern per major boundary.
   161	
   162	Examples: "debug webhook failures", "debug pipeline stage failures",
   163	"diagnose auth/permission issues", "debug background job failures"
   164	
   165	### Category 4 — Deploy/release patterns
   166	
   167	Only generate if `context/setup.md` reveals non-trivial deployment.
   168	
   169	Examples: "deploy to staging", "rollback a release", "update environment config",
   170	"run database migration in production"

# Iteration 011: GAP ANALYSIS - MISSING FEATURES

## Focus
GAP ANALYSIS - MISSING FEATURES: What entirely NEW features should we build that we haven't considered?

## Findings
### Finding 1: We should build a first-class `memory_sync_packet` workflow that turns drift findings into repair-ready briefs
- **Source**: [external/src/sync/index.ts:29](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L29), [external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts:7](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L7), [memory-crud-health.ts:419](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts#L419), [memory-crud-health.ts:454](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts#L454)
- **What it does**: Mex groups issues by file, lets the operator sync errors first or include warnings, builds one combined repair brief, injects the current file content, issue list, nearby filesystem candidates, and relevant git diff, then re-checks in a loop until drift is resolved or the user stops. Our `memory_health` auto-repair is limited to DB/runtime integrity actions like FTS rebuild, trigger-cache refresh, and orphan cleanup.
- **Why it matters for us**: We already detect many problems, but we still make operators manually translate diagnostics into fix plans. A repair-packet surface for stale memories/spec docs would turn health output into a low-token remediation workflow instead of a human reconstruction exercise.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: We should add a durable project router surface with explicit “Working / Not Built / Known Issues” state
- **Source**: [external/README.md:178](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L178), [external/ROUTER.md:26](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/ROUTER.md#L26), [startup-brief.ts:88](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/code-graph/startup-brief.ts#L88), [session-bootstrap.ts:94](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L94)
- **What it does**: Mex keeps a durable router file that re-grounds every session with current project state plus a task-routing table. Our startup surfaces are strong, but they are mostly transport/runtime summaries: graph freshness, session continuity, and next actions, not an explicit, durable model of product state and where work should route.
- **Why it matters for us**: Retrieval answers “what memories match this question,” but not “what is currently true about this project.” A small, durable project-state board would reduce cold-start ambiguity and make stale-state drift visible in a way memory search alone does not.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: We should create a first-class task-pattern registry instead of only storing episodic memories
- **Source**: [external/AGENTS.md:37](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/AGENTS.md#L37), [external/CLAUDE.md:34](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/CLAUDE.md#L34), [external/src/pattern/index.ts:6](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/pattern/index.ts#L6), [external/patterns/README.md:5](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/patterns/README.md#L5), [generate-context.js:81](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js#L81), [post-save-review.ts:901](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/post-save-review.ts#L901)
- **What it does**: Mex distinguishes repeatable project playbooks from raw history. Patterns encode Context, Steps, Gotchas, Verify, Debug, and scaffold updates, and the system explicitly grows that registry after real tasks. Our save path captures rich session data and aggressively reviews memory hygiene, but it still produces memory artifacts, not reusable task playbooks.
- **Why it matters for us**: We are good at remembering what happened. We are weaker at crystallizing “how to do this task correctly next time” into a durable, reusable unit. A pattern registry would convert repeated session knowledge into compact operator guidance and cut repeated rediscovery.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 4: We should build a repo bootstrap brief generator for cold-start onboarding and scaffold seeding
- **Source**: [external/src/scanner/index.ts:20](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/scanner/index.ts#L20), [external/src/scanner/manifest.ts:12](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/scanner/manifest.ts#L12), [external/src/scanner/entry-points.ts:35](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/scanner/entry-points.ts#L35), [external/src/scanner/tooling.ts:5](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/scanner/tooling.ts#L5), [startup-brief.ts:131](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/code-graph/startup-brief.ts#L131)
- **What it does**: Mex builds a compact structured brief from manifest data, entry points, top-level folder categories, detected tooling, and truncated README content before asking AI to populate scaffold files. Our startup brief gives structural highlights and continuity, but not a comparable repo brief centered on manifest/tooling/README facts.
- **Why it matters for us**: This would give us a low-cost “authoritative repo snapshot” for research, onboarding, and initial memory/scaffold seeding, especially in repos where no prior memory exists or where session continuity is thin.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 5: We should add reverse coverage audits that detect live repo knowledge missing from memory/spec surfaces
- **Source**: [external/src/drift/checkers/script-coverage.ts:26](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/script-coverage.ts#L26), [external/src/drift/checkers/command.ts:5](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/command.ts#L5), [external/src/drift/checkers/dependency.ts:38](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/dependency.ts#L38), [post-save-review.ts:807](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/post-save-review.ts#L807), [validate-memory-quality.ts:873](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/validate-memory-quality.ts#L873), [memory-crud-health.ts:382](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts#L382)
- **What it does**: Mex checks the opposite direction from most doc lint: not just “does the scaffold claim something false,” but also “does the live repo expose scripts/commands/dependencies that the scaffold never mentions.” Our current quality checks are strong on saved-memory metadata, trigger drift, alias divergence, and DB integrity, but not on repo-to-knowledge coverage.
- **Why it matters for us**: This would catch silent knowledge debt: new operational commands, dependencies, or repo affordances that exist in reality but never become retrievable memory or documentation. That gap is operationally expensive because nothing looks broken until someone needs the missing knowledge.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

- [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts)
- [external/src/pattern/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/pattern/index.ts)
- [external/patterns/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/patterns/README.md)
- [memory-crud-health.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts)
- [startup-brief.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/code-graph/startup-brief.ts)
- [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts)
- [causal-edges.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/causal-edges.ts)
- [post-save-review.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/post-save-review.ts)
- [validate-memory-quality.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/validate-memory-quality.ts)
- [generate-context.js](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js)

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.84
- Questions addressed: which Mex capabilities are genuinely absent from Spec Kit Memory, whether Mex adds operator workflows beyond drift linting, whether we lack a durable router/state layer, whether we lack reusable task-pattern artifacts, whether we lack a cold-start repo brief, whether we lack reality-to-knowledge coverage audits.
- Questions answered: the main missed opportunities are not new retrieval channels but new operator products around retrieval: repair packets, a project router/current-state board, a reusable task-pattern registry, a repo bootstrap brief generator, and reverse coverage audits for commands/dependencies/scripts.

## Reflection
- What worked: tracing Mex from `sync` and `scanner` inward, then mapping each operator-facing loop against our `memory_health`, startup, and save-path surfaces, made the missing-feature set much clearer than checker-by-checker parity alone.
- What did not work: the semantic code-search pass did not complete, so the absence claims had to be verified manually through direct source reads; I also did not broaden into non-memory Spec Kit validators because this iteration was specifically about the external memory system comparison.

## Recommended Next Focus
Compare Modus Memory and Mnemosyne specifically for two things: whether either system has an equivalent to Mex’s reusable task-pattern layer, and whether either one already turns drift/health findings into operator-facing repair packets. If neither does, these five features are likely original follow-on product directions for Spec Kit Memory.

# Iteration 011: GAP ANALYSIS - MISSING FEATURES

## Focus
GAP ANALYSIS - MISSING FEATURES: What entirely NEW features should we build that we haven't considered?

## Findings
### Finding 1: We should build a first-class `memory_sync_packet` workflow that turns drift findings into repair-ready briefs
- **Source**: [external/src/sync/index.ts:29](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L29), [external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts:7](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L7), [memory-crud-health.ts:419](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts#L419), [memory-crud-health.ts:454](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts#L454)
- **What it does**: Mex groups issues by file, lets the operator sync errors first or include warnings, builds one combined repair brief, injects the current file content, issue list, nearby filesystem candidates, and relevant git diff, then re-checks in a loop until drift is resolved or the user stops. Our `memory_health` auto-repair is limited to DB/runtime integrity actions like FTS rebuild, trigger-cache refresh, and orphan cleanup.
- **Why it matters for us**: We already detect many problems, but we still make operators manually translate diagnostics into fix plans. A repair-packet surface for stale memories/spec docs would turn health output into a low-token remediation workflow instead of a human reconstruction exercise.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: We should add a durable project router surface with explicit “Working / Not Built / Known Issues” state
- **Source**: [external/README.md:178](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L178), [external/ROUTER.md:26](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/ROUTER.md#L26), [startup-brief.ts:88](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/code-graph/startup-brief.ts#L88), [session-bootstrap.ts:94](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L94)
- **What it does**: Mex keeps a durable router file that re-grounds every session with current project state plus a task-routing table. Our startup surfaces are strong, but they are mostly transport/runtime summaries: graph freshness, session continuity, and next actions, not an explicit, durable model of product state and where work should route.
- **Why it matters for us**: Retrieval answers “what memories match this question,” but not “what is currently true about this project.” A small, durable project-state board would reduce cold-start ambiguity and make stale-state drift visible in a way memory search alone does not.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: We should create a first-class task-pattern registry instead of only storing episodic memories
- **Source**: [external/AGENTS.md:37](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/AGENTS.md#L37), [external/CLAUDE.md:34](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/CLAUDE.md#L34), [external/src/pattern/index.ts:6](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/pattern/index.ts#L6), [external/patterns/README.md:5](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/patterns/README.md#L5), [generate-context.js:81](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js#L81), [post-save-review.ts:901](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/post-save-review.ts#L901)
- **What it does**: Mex distinguishes repeatable project playbooks from raw history. Patterns encode Context, Steps, Gotchas, Verify, Debug, and scaffold updates, and the system explicitly grows that registry after real tasks. Our save path captures rich session data and aggressively reviews memory hygiene, but it still produces memory artifacts, not reusable task playbooks.
- **Why it matters for us**: We are good at remembering what happened. We are weaker at crystallizing “how to do this task correctly next time” into a durable, reusable unit. A pattern registry would convert repeated session knowledge into compact operator guidance and cut repeated rediscovery.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 4: We should build a repo bootstrap brief generator for cold-start onboarding and scaffold seeding
- **Source**: [external/src/scanner/index.ts:20](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/scanner/index.ts#L20), [external/src/scanner/manifest.ts:12](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/scanner/manifest.ts#L12), [external/src/scanner/entry-points.ts:35](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/scanner/entry-points.ts#L35), [external/src/scanner/tooling.ts:5](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/scanner/tooling.ts#L5), [startup-brief.ts:131](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/code-graph/startup-brief.ts#L131)
- **What it does**: Mex builds a compact structured brief from manifest data, entry points, top-level folder categories, detected tooling, and truncated README content before asking AI to populate scaffold files. Our startup brief gives structural highlights and continuity, but not a comparable repo brief centered on manifest/tooling/README facts.
- **Why it matters for us**: This would give us a low-cost “authoritative repo snapshot” for research, onboarding, and initial memory/scaffold seeding, especially in repos where no prior memory exists or where session continuity is thin.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 5: We should add reverse coverage audits that detect live repo knowledge missing from memory/spec surfaces
- **Source**: [external/src/drift/checkers/script-coverage.ts:26](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/script-coverage.ts#L26), [external/src/drift/checkers/command.ts:5](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/command.ts#L5), [external/src/drift/checkers/dependency.ts:38](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/dependency.ts#L38), [post-save-review.ts:807](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/post-save-review.ts#L807), [validate-memory-quality.ts:873](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/validate-memory-quality.ts#L873), [memory-crud-health.ts:382](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts#L382)
- **What it does**: Mex checks the opposite direction from most doc lint: not just “does the scaffold claim something false,” but also “does the live repo expose scripts/commands/dependencies that the scaffold never mentions.” Our current quality checks are strong on saved-memory metadata, trigger drift, alias divergence, and DB integrity, but not on repo-to-knowledge coverage.
- **Why it matters for us**: This would catch silent knowledge debt: new operational commands, dependencies, or repo affordances that exist in reality but never become retrievable memory or documentation. That gap is operationally expensive because nothing looks broken until someone needs the missing knowledge.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.84
- Questions addressed: which Mex capabilities are genuinely absent from Spec Kit Memory, whether Mex adds operator workflows beyond drift linting, whether we lack a durable router/state layer, whether we lack reusable task-pattern artifacts, whether we lack a cold-start repo brief, whether we lack reality-to-knowledge coverage audits.
- Questions answered: the main missed opportunities are not new retrieval channels but new operator products around retrieval: repair packets, a project router/current-state board, a reusable task-pattern registry, a repo bootstrap brief generator, and reverse coverage audits for commands/dependencies/scripts.

## Reflection
- What worked: tracing Mex from `sync` and `scanner` inward, then mapping each operator-facing loop against our `memory_health`, startup, and save-path surfaces, made the missing-feature set much clearer than checker-by-checker parity alone.
- What did not work: the semantic code-search pass did not complete, so the absence claims had to be verified manually through direct source reads; I also did not broaden into non-memory Spec Kit validators because this iteration was specifically about the external memory system comparison.

## Recommended Next Focus
Compare Modus Memory and Mnemosyne specifically for two things: whether either system has an equivalent to Mex’s reusable task-pattern layer, and whether either one already turns drift/health findings into operator-facing repair packets. If neither does, these five features are likely original follow-on product directions for Spec Kit Memory.
